title: Recovering Sparse Neural Connectivity from Partial Measurements
target_venue: NeurIPS Workshop on Symmetry and Geometry in Neural Representations (NeurReps)
core_question: Can we recover the connectivity matrix of a neural circuit from sparse, partial measurements across multiple recording sessions?
core_claim: Covariance-based weight estimation with Granger-causality refinement can recover sparse neural connectivity from partial measurements, but there is a fundamental tradeoff between stimulation-driven identifiability and intrinsic dynamics richness.

hypotheses.statement: The covariance estimator W_hat = Cov(x_{t+1}, x_t) @ pinv(Cov(x_t, x_t)) recovers W better than chance baselines
hypotheses.metric: Frobenius error normalized by network size

hypotheses.statement: Recovery accuracy improves with measurement fraction (more neurons observed simultaneously)
There exists a tradeoff: higher stimulation gain improves identifiability but disrupts intrinsic dynamics
hypotheses.metric: Recovery error and dynamics quality vs stimulation gain

hypotheses.statement: Granger-causality refinement improves precision and recall of edge detection
hypotheses.metric: Precision/recall of nonzero entries in W

hypotheses.statement: The linear approximation phi(x) ≈ x degrades gracefully under model mismatch

figures_needed.description: Problem setup schematic (network with measured/unmeasured/CPG nodes)
True vs estimated W heatmaps at varying measurement density
Recovery error vs stimulation gain (the core tradeoff curve)
Scaling with network size and recording duration
C. elegans application: recovery on biologically realistic topology

acceptance_criteria.scope: Research question is specific, falsifiable, and novel
acceptance_criteria.literature: Key prior works cited, positioning clear, gaps identified
acceptance_criteria.theory: Derivations correct, assumptions stated, bounds provided
acceptance_criteria.experiment: Experiments test stated claims, baselines included, reproducible
acceptance_criteria.analyze: Figures clear, results support claims, failure cases discussed
acceptance_criteria.write: Writing clear and concise, paper self-contained, meets format
acceptance_criteria.review: Reviewer would accept, weaknesses addressed, rebuttal ready
